SEARCH

Class Hospital

java.lang.Object
Hospital
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Hospital extends Object implements Serializable
Business class representing a hospital in the emergency dispatch system. A hospital contains all information required for patient assignment and emergency service management:
  • a unique identifier;
  • a display name;
  • a geographic position;
  • a maximum capacity;
  • a current occupancy level;
  • the medical services available.
This class implements Serializable so that hospitals can be saved and restored using the persistence layer of the application. Two hospitals are considered equal when they share the same identifier.

Class type: Business class (Model layer).

Version:
1.0
Author:
Maïssa Tirsane, Anas Chokri, Iyed Souissi, Valery Vo-Van
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • Hospital

      public Hospital(String id, String name, Coordinate position, int maxCapacity)
      Creates a new hospital. The hospital is initially empty and no medical service is registered by default.
      Parameters:
      id - unique hospital identifier
      name - hospital name
      position - hospital geographic position
      maxCapacity - maximum number of patients that can be admitted

  • Method Details

    • getSaturationRate

      public double getSaturationRate()
      Computes the hospital saturation rate. The returned value is between 0 and 1 and is used for statistics, patient assignment visualization and hospital status monitoring.
      Returns:
      saturation rate of the hospital

    • isSaturated

      public boolean isSaturated()
      Checks whether the hospital has reached its maximum capacity.
      Returns:
      true if the hospital is saturated

    • updateCapacity

      public void updateCapacity(int currentCapacity)
      Updates the current occupancy level. The value is automatically clamped between 0 and the maximum capacity in order to preserve object consistency.
      Parameters:
      currentCapacity - new occupancy level

    • addService

      public void addService(HospitalServiceType service)
      Adds a medical service to the hospital. A Set is used to automatically prevent duplicates.
      Parameters:
      service - medical service to add

    • hasService

      public boolean hasService(HospitalServiceType service)
      Checks whether the hospital provides a specific service. This method is mainly used by AssignmentService when searching for the most appropriate hospital for a patient.
      Parameters:
      service - requested medical service
      Returns:
      true if the service is available

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Returns the hospital identifier.
      Returns:
      hospital identifier

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the hospital name.
      Returns:
      hospital name

    • getPosition

      public Coordinate getPosition()
      Returns the hospital position.
      Returns:
      geographic position

    • getMaxCapacity

      public int getMaxCapacity()
      Returns the maximum capacity.
      Returns:
      maximum capacity

    • getCurrentCapacity

      public int getCurrentCapacity()
      Returns the current occupancy level.
      Returns:
      current number of admitted patients

    • getServices

      public Set<HospitalServiceType> getServices()
      Returns a defensive copy of the service set. Returning a copy prevents external classes from modifying the internal collection directly.
      Returns:
      copy of available services

    • updatePosition

      public void updatePosition(Coordinate newPosition)
      Updates the geographic position of the hospital. This method is notably used when a hospital is moved through the graphical interface.
      Parameters:
      newPosition - new hospital position

    • admitPatient

      public boolean admitPatient()
      Attempts to admit a new patient. The occupancy level is increased only if the hospital is not already saturated.
      Returns:
      true if the patient was successfully admitted

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a complete textual representation of the hospital. Mainly used for debugging and console outputs.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      hospital description

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Compares this hospital with another object. Two hospitals are considered equal when they share the same unique identifier.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - object to compare
      Returns:
      true if both hospitals have the same identifier

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Generates a hash code consistent with equals(). This implementation allows Hospital objects to be safely stored in hash-based collections such as HashSet and HashMap.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      hospital hash code
